content: Just as the high school literacy concept of reflexion frames the practice as a structured guided activity to help students refine their writing by stepping back to examine their own output, current LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this educational scaffolding to design more effective reflection pipelines, rather than relying on unstructured self-evaluation. Structured reflexion exercises that were developed to teach human learners metacognition can be adapted as prompt templates to improve LLM self-reflection consistency and accuracy.
